nodes.context: A 1950s Puerto Rican street gang in New York City, involved in various criminal activities.
A street gang from which some members broke away to form the Mau Maus.
A dominant local gang in Brooklyn that supported the formation of the Mau Maus.
A rival gang that had conflicts with the Mau Maus.
An enemy gang of the Mau Maus.
A gang led by former Mau Maus member Salvador Agrón.

edges.evidence_quote: Some members of a street gang called the Apaches broke away and created the Mau Maus
edges.context: The Mau Maus were formed by members who broke away from the Apaches.

edges.evidence_quote: They also asked permission from a rival gang called the Chaplains to start a Puerto Rican gang in the area. Eventually the gang was called Mau Mau Chaplains.
edges.context: The Mau Maus sought and received permission from the Chaplains to form, and initially used the name Mau Mau Chaplains.

edges.evidence_quote: Around January 1958 a member of the rival Bishop gang and a candy store owner were stabbed to death by some of the Mau Mau gang, supposedly in retaliation for the Bishops' killing of Mannie Durango
edges.context: The Mau Maus retaliated against the Bishops for killing one of their members.

edges.evidence_quote: shot and killed Anthony Lavonchino, a member of the Sand Street Angels, one of the Mau Maus' enemies
edges.context: The Mau Maus killed a member of the Sand Street Angels, an enemy gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Salvador Agrón, a member of the Mau Maus, later led another gang called the Vampires
edges.context: A former Mau Maus member founded and led the Vampires.
